preamble: The transformation from a voiceless velar fricative ([x]) to a voiceless aspirated velar plosive ([kʰ]) is a process of fortition, specifically involving the stopping of the fricative and the addition of aspiration. This shift is less common than lenition (plosive to fricative) but is attested in certain languages.

examples.note: The initial /x/ of the root 'xar' (fertile) becomes /kʰ/ in the affixed form 'ɔ.kʰar' (non-fertile).
The initial /x/ of the root 'xɔt' (honest) becomes /kʰ/ in the affixed form 'ɔ.kʰɔt'.

sources: Hyslop, G., & Tshering, K. (2009). Preliminary Notes on Dakpa (Tawang Monpa). North East Indian Linguistics, 2.
